original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Procedural Sedation in the Emergency Department
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE credentialing, medicine and facility policy govern local use
original_registry_entry.population: Adults and children, excluding neonates, undergoing procedural sedation in an emergency department.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Emergency department procedural sedation governance, pre-sedation assessment, monitoring and post-sedation review.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting use of a sedation proforma, clinician assessment of patient and procedure factors, staffing and environment, monitoring, adverse events and clinician post-sedation review. Pharmacological agents and doses were explicitly excluded from mappings.

exact_sections.heading: Summary, governance and auditable sedation record
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports an auditable sedation proforma, competency, staffing, equipment, adverse-event reporting and recording without selecting a sedative or dose.

exact_sections.heading: Patient and procedure factors, monitoring and post-sedation review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-assessed patient/procedure factors, airway and aspiration risk, planned level, monitoring, adverse events and clinician review after sedation. Medicine and dose tables are excluded.

title: Procedural Sedation in the Emergency Department
jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE credentialing, medicine and facility policy govern local use
population: Adults and children, excluding neonates, undergoing procedural sedation in an emergency department.
intended_setting: Emergency department procedural sedation governance, pre-sedation assessment, monitoring and post-sedation review.
access_limitations: One or more PDF tables require structured extraction
